Create a simple Aspire application using a default template.\\n2. Add a sql server resource\\n3. Inside the ConfigureInfrastructure callback, add the policy, then just do `aspire publish` to get the generated bicep\\n\\nSample snippet - add this in your Aspire App Host\\n\\n\\u0026gt; You\\u0026#39;ll need to have a dev environment setup to run Aspire projects. If all you\\u0026#39;re doing is executing `aspire publish` the Aspire CLI would be sufficient. If you\\u0026#39;re going to run the project you\\u0026#39;ll need Docker, at least for how the below code is written.\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar azureSql = builder.AddAzureSqlServer(\\u0026#34;azuresql\\u0026#34;)\\n  .RunAsContainer(c =\\u0026gt; c.WithLifetime(ContainerLifetime.Persistent))\\n  .ConfigureInfrastructure(infra =\\u0026gt;\\n    {\\n        var server = infra.GetProvisionableResources().OfType().Single();\\n        var auditingPolicy = new SqlServerBlobAuditingPolicy(\\u0026#34;auditing\\u0026#34;)\\n        {\\n            Parent = server,\\n            State = BlobAuditingPolicyState.Enabled,\\n            IsAzureMonitorTargetEnabled = true,\\n            AuditActionsAndGroups =\\n            {\\n                \\u0026#34;BATCH_COMPLETED_GROUP\\u0026#34;,\\n                \\u0026#34;SUCCESSFUL_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP\\u0026#34;,\\n                \\u0026#34;FAILED_DATABASE_AUTHENTICATION_GROUP\\u0026#34;,\\n            },\\n        };\\n\\n        // Leaving the below code commented produces the invalid bicep. Uncomment to see the error go away in bicep\\n        // var nameValue = new BicepValue(\\u0026#34;default\\u0026#34;);\\n        // ((IBicepValue)nameValue).Self = new BicepValueReference(auditingPolicy, \\u0026#34;Name\\u0026#34;, [\\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34;]);\\n        // auditingPolicy.ProvisionableProperties[\\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34;] = nameValue;\\n\\n        infra.Add(auditingPolicy);\\n    });\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n.NET SDK:\\n Version:           10.0.107\\n Commit:            b16286c228\\n Workload version:  10.0.100-manifests.ef86bccd\\n MSBuild version:   18.0.11+b16286c22\\n\\nRuntime Environment:\\n OS Name:     Windows\\n OS Version:  10.0.26200\\n OS Platform: Windows\\n RID:         win-x64\\n Base Path:   C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk\\\\10.0.107\\\\\\n\\n.NET workloads installed:\\nThere are no installed workloads to display.\\nConfigured to use workload sets when installing new manifests.\\nNo workload sets are installed.
